input_preview:
user_query: 
symbol: 
task_family: golden_bookmark_evidence_review
claim: NVIDIA and Google are leading 800 VDC adoption ahead of schedule, with small-volume shipments starting around Q3 2026; Vertiv and Delta are relevant suppliers.
draft_memo: 
evidence_summary: ["NVIDIA describes 800 VDC as next-generation power distribution for AI factories and lists electrical ecosystem partners including Delta and Vertiv.", "Vertiv says its 800 VDC power portfolio is planned for release in the second half of 2026 to support NVIDIA Rubin Ultra platforms.", "Taipei Times reports Delta plans small-volume 800V HVDC shipments to NVIDIA next quarter and says a major search-engine company has begun deploying +/-400V architecture."]
cited_evidence_ids: 
verdict: partial_support
source: https://x.com/SUOHA_AI

user_query: 
symbol: 
task_family: golden_bookmark_evidence_review
claim: Marvell frames next-generation AI infrastructure performance around compute, memory, and especially connectivity; MRVL/Marvell official material supports connectivity as a core AI-infra bottleneck.
draft_memo: 
evidence_summary: ["Marvell COMPUTEX 2026 page says Matt Murphy delivered The Future of AI Scaling Depends on Connectivity and discussed critical links in modern AI data-center infrastructure.", "Marvell says AI scaling depends on how efficiently data moves across accelerators, servers, racks, campuses, and geographically distributed data centers."]
cited_evidence_ids: 
verdict: supports
source: https://x.com/Kay2289123/status/2070238633114869795
